test.name: Surface collateral records using record_record_matches after extraction
test.description: After extracting a 1850 census record for Patrick Flynn, the user asks what other records FamilySearch has matched to this same persona. Tests that record-extraction calls record_record_matches and surfaces the two pending collateral matches (1860 census, Pennsylvania death certificate) with their confidence levels so the user can decide what to extract next.

judge_context: Should call record_record_matches with id MXHY-TP4
Should report the two pending collateral matches: the 1860 census (confidence 4, score 0.88) and Pennsylvania Death Certificates (confidence 3, score 0.65)
Should highlight the 1860 census as the higher-priority follow-up given its confidence 4 score
Should still extract assertions from the 1850 census record before or alongside checking collateral records

Informant: for name/age/birthplace, use 'unknown household member' with reasoning (e.g., 'likely a parent' for a child) and proximity 'household_member'. For residence, 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness' is acceptable. Use informant_bias_notes to explain reasoning.
